3: ##

5: # LLM (Language Model) providers.

7: # @abstract

11:   ##
12:   # @param [String, nil] key
13:   #  The secret key for authentication
14:   # @param [String] host
15:   #  The host address of the LLM provider
16:   # @param [Integer] port
17:   #  The port number
18:   # @param [Integer] timeout
19:   #  The number of seconds to wait for a response
20:   # @param [Boolean] ssl
21:   #  Whether to use SSL for the connection
22:   # @param [String] base_path
23:   #  Optional base path prefix for HTTP API routes.
24:   # @param [Boolean] persistent
25:   #  Whether to use a persistent connection.
26:   #  Requires the net-http-persistent gem.
27:   # @param [LLM::Transport, Class, nil] transport
28:   #  Optional override with any {LLM::Transport} instance or subclass.
29:   def initialize(key:, host:, port: 443, timeout: 60, ssl: true, base_path: "", persistent: false, transport: nil)
30:     @key = key
31:     @host = host
32:     @port = port
33:     @timeout = timeout
34:     @ssl = ssl
35:     @base_path = LLM::Utils.normalize_base_path(base_path)
36:     @base_uri = URI("#{ssl ? "https" : "http"}://#{host}:#{port}/")
37:     @headers = {"User-Agent" => "llm.rb v#{LLM::VERSION}"}
38:     @transport = LLM::Transport::Utils.resolve_transport(host:, port:, timeout:, ssl:, transport:, persistent:)
39:     @monitor = Monitor.new
40:   end

43:   # Returns an inspection of the provider object
44:   # @return [String]
45:   # @note The secret key is redacted in inspect for security reasons

47:     "#<#{LLM::Utils.object_id(self)} @key=[REDACTED] @transport=#{transport.inspect} @tracer=#{tracer.inspect}>"
48:   end

57:   end

3: ##
4: # The {LLM::Registry LLM::Registry} class provides a small API over
5: # provider model data. It exposes model metadata such as pricing,
6: # capabilities, modalities, and limits from the registry files
7: # stored under `data/`. The data is provided by https://models.dev
8: # and shipped with llm.rb.
9: class LLM::Registry
10:   @root = File.join(__dir__, "..", "..")
11: 
12:   ##
13:   # @raise [LLM::Error]
14:   #  Might raise an error
15:   # @param [Symbol]
16:   #  A provider name
17:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
18:   def self.for(name)
19:     path = File.join @root, "data", "#{name}.json"
20:     if File.file?(path)
21:       new LLM.json.load(File.binread(path))
22:     else
23:       raise LLM::NoSuchRegistryError, "no registry found for #{name}"
24:     end
25:   end
26: 
27:   ##
28:   # @param [Hash] blob
29:   #  A model registry
30:   # @return [LLM::Registry]
31:   def initialize(blob)
32:     @registry = LLM::Object.from(blob)
33:     @models = @registry.models
34:   end
35: 
36:   ##
37:   # @return [LLM::Object]
38:   #  Returns model costs
39:   def cost(model:)
40:     lookup(model:).cost
41:   end
42: 
43:   ##
44:   # @return [LLM::Object]
45:   #  Returns model modalities
46:   def modalities(model:)
47:     lookup(model:).modalities
48:   end
49: 
50:   ##
51:   # @return [LLM::Object]
52:   #  Returns model limits such as the context window size
53:   def limit(model:)
54:     lookup(model:).limit
55:   end
56: 
57:   private
58: 
59:   def lookup(model:)
60:     if @models.key?(model)
61:       @models[model]
62:     else
63:       patterns = {/-\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2}$/ => "", /\A(gpt-.*)-\d{4}$/ => "\\1"}
64:       fallback = find_map(patterns) { model.dup.sub!(_1, _2) } || "none"
65:       if @models.key?(fallback)
66:         @models[fallback]
67:       else
68:         raise LLM::NoSuchModelError, "no such model: #{model} (fallback: #{fallback})"
69:       end
70:     end
71:   end
72: 
73:   ##
74:   # Similar to #{find} but returns the block's return value
75:   # @return [Object, nil]
76:   def find_map(pair)
77:     result = nil
78:     pair.each_pair { break if result = yield(_1, _2) }
79:     result
80:   end
81: end

212:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))

215:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
216:       if params[:stream]

224:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: adapt(messages, mode: :complete).flatten}.merge!(params))
225:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(completions_path, headers)
226:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # {LLM::Response LLM::Response} is the normalized base shape for
6:   # provider and endpoint responses in llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # Provider calls return an instance of this class, then extend it
9:   # with provider-, endpoint-, or context-specific modules so response
10:   # handling can share one common surface without flattening away
11:   # specialized behavior.
12:   #
13:   # The normalized response keeps the transport response available
14:   # through {#res}. When the default net/http transport is in use,
15:   # {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP
16:   # LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP} keeps the
17:   # original {Net::HTTPResponse Net::HTTPResponse} available through
18:   # its own {LLM::Transport::Response::HTTP#res #res}.
19:   class Response
20:     require "json"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # Returns the HTTP response
24:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
25:     attr_reader :res
26: 
27:     ##
28:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
29:     #  HTTP response
30:     # @return [LLM::Response]

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM::OpenAI::RequestAdapter
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class Completion
7:     ##
8:     # @param [LLM::Message, Hash] message
9:     #  The message to format
10:     def initialize(message)
11:       @message = message
12:     end
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Adapts the message for the OpenAI chat completions API
16:     # @return [Hash]
17:     def adapt
18:       catch(:abort) do
19:         if Hash === message
20:           {role: message[:role], content: adapt_content(message[:content])}
21:         elsif message.tool_call?
22:           {role: message.role, content: nil, tool_calls: message.extra[:original_tool_calls]}
23:         else
24:           adapt_message
25:         end
26:       end
27:     end
28: 
29:     private
30: 
31:     def adapt_message
32:       case content
33:       when Array
34:         adapt_array
35:       else
36:         {role: message.role, content: adapt_content(content)}
37:       end
38:     end
39: 
40:     def adapt_array
41:       if content.empty?
42:         nil
43:       elsif returns.any?
44:         returns.map { {role: "tool", tool_call_id: _1.id, content: LLM.json.dump(_1.value)} }
45:       else
46:         {role: message.role, content: content.flat_map { adapt_content(_1) }}
47:       end
48:     end
49: 
50:     def adapt_content(content)
51:       case content
52:       when LLM::Object
53:         adapt_object(content)
54:       when String
55:         [{type: :text, text: content.to_s}]
56:       when LLM::Response
57:         adapt_remote_file(content)
58:       when LLM::Message
59:         adapt_content(content.content)
60:       when LLM::Function::Return
61:         throw(:abort, {role: "tool", tool_call_id: content.id, content: LLM.json.dump(content.value)})
62:       else
63:         prompt_error!(content)
64:       end
65:     end
66: 
67:     def adapt_object(object)
68:       case object.kind
69:       when :image_url
70:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: object.value}}]
71:       when :local_file
72:         adapt_local_file(object.value)
73:       when :remote_file
74:         adapt_remote_file(object.value)
75:       else
76:         prompt_error!(object)
77:       end
78:     end
79: 
80:     def adapt_local_file(file)
81:       if file.image?
82:         [{type: :image_url, image_url: {url: file.to_data_uri}}]
83:       else
84:         [{type: :file, file: {filename: file.basename, file_data: file.to_data_uri}}]
85:       end
86:     end
87: 
88:     def adapt_remote_file(file)
89:       if file.file?
90:         [{type: :file, file: {file_id: file.id}}]
91:       else
92:         prompt_error!(file)
93:       end
94:     end
95: 
96:     def prompt_error!(content)
97:       if LLM::Object === content
98:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given LLM::Object with kind '#{content.kind}' is not " \
99:                                 "supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
100:       else
101:         raise LLM::PromptError, "The given object (an instance of #{content.class}) " \
102:                                 "is not supported by the OpenAI chat completions API."
103:       end
104:     end
105: 
106:     def message = @message
107:     def content = message.content
108:     def returns = content.grep(LLM::Function::Return)
109:   end
110: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     EMPTY_HASH = {}.freeze
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
11:     # @return [Hash]
12:     attr_reader :body
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: {}}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
22:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
23:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
24:     end
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [Hash] chunk
28:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::Chunk]
29:     def parse!(chunk)
30:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
31:     end
32: 
33:     ##
34:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
35:     # @return [void]
36:     def free
37:       @emits.clear
38:     end
39: 
40:     private
41: 
42:     def merge!(chunk)
43:       chunk.each do |key, value|
44:         if key == "choices"
45:           @body["choices"] ||= []
46:           merge_choices!(value)
47:         else
48:           @body[key] = value
49:         end
50:       end
51:     end
52: 
53:     def merge_choices!(choices)
54:       body_choices = @body["choices"]
55:       choices.each do |choice|
56:         index = choice["index"]
57:         delta = choice["delta"] || EMPTY_HASH
58:         target_message = if body_choice = body_choices[index]
59:           body_choice["message"]
60:         else
61:           body_choices[index] = {"message" => {"role" => "assistant"}}
62:           body_choices[index]["message"]
63:         end
64:         merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
65:       end
66:     end
67: 
68:     def merge_delta!(target_message, delta)
69:       if delta.length == 1
70:         merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
71:       elsif content = delta["content"]
72:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
73:           target_content << content
74:         else
75:           target_message["content"] = content
76:         end
77:         emit_content(content)
78:       elsif reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
79:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
80:           target_reasoning << reasoning
81:         else
82:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
83:         end
84:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
85:       elsif tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
86:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
87:       end
88:       return if delta.length <= 1
89:       delta.each do |key, value|
90:         next if value.nil? || key == "content" || key == "reasoning_content" || key == "tool_calls"
91:         target_message[key] = value
92:       end
93:     end
94: 
95:     def merge_single_delta!(target_message, delta)
96:       if content = delta["content"]
97:         if target_content = target_message["content"]
98:           target_content << content
99:         else
100:           target_message["content"] = content
101:         end
102:         emit_content(content)
103:         return
104:       end
105:       if reasoning = delta["reasoning_content"]
106:         if target_reasoning = target_message["reasoning_content"]
107:           target_reasoning << reasoning
108:         else
109:           target_message["reasoning_content"] = reasoning
110:         end
111:         emit_reasoning_content(reasoning)
112:         return
113:       end
114:       if tool_calls = delta["tool_calls"]
115:         merge_tools!(target_message, tool_calls)
116:       end
117:     end
118: 
119:     def merge_tools!(target, tools)
120:       target["tool_calls"] ||= []
121:       tools.each.with_index do |toola, index|
122:         tindex = toola["index"]
123:         tindex = index unless Integer === tindex && tindex >= 0
124:         toolb = target["tool_calls"][tindex]
125:         functiona = toola["function"]
126:         functionb = toolb && toolb["function"]
127:         if functiona && functionb
128:           # Append to existing function arguments
129:           merge_function!(functionb, functiona)
130:         else
131:           target["tool_calls"][tindex] = toola
132:         end
133:         emit_tool(target["tool_calls"][tindex], tindex)
134:       end
135:     end
136: 
137:     def merge_function!(target, source)
138:       if arguments = source["arguments"]
139:         if target_arguments = target["arguments"]
140:           target_arguments << arguments
141:         else
142:           target["arguments"] = arguments
143:         end
144:       end
145:       if name = source["name"]
146:         if target_name = target["name"]
147:           target_name << name
148:         else
149:           target["name"] = name
150:         end
151:       end
152:       return if source.length <= 2
153:       source.each do |func_key, func_value|
154:         next if func_key == "arguments" || func_key == "name"
155:         target[func_key] ||= +""
156:         target[func_key] << func_value
157:       end
158:     end
159: 
160:     def emit_content(value)
161:       if @can_emit_content
162:         @stream.on_content(value)
163:       elsif @can_push_content
164:         @stream << value
165:       end
166:     end
167: 
168:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
169:       if @can_emit_reasoning_content
170:         @stream.on_reasoning_content(value)
171:       end
172:     end
173: 
174:     def emit_tool(tool, tindex)
175:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
176:       return if @emits[:tools][tindex]
177:       function = tool["function"]
178:       return unless function && tool["id"] && function["name"]
179:       return unless arguments_complete?(function["arguments"])
180:       arguments = parse_arguments(function["arguments"])
181:       return unless arguments
182:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
183:       @emits[:tools][tindex] = true
184:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
185:     end
186: 
187:     def resolve_tool(tool, function, arguments)
188:       registered = @stream.__find__(function["name"])
189:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(function["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
190:         fn.id = tool["id"]
191:         fn.arguments = arguments
192:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
193:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
194:       end
195:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
196:     end
197: 
198:     def parse_arguments(arguments)
199:       return nil if arguments.to_s.empty?
200:       parsed = LLM.json.load(arguments)
201:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : nil
202:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
203:       nil
204:     end
205: 
206:     def arguments_complete?(arguments)
207:       value = arguments.to_s.rstrip
208:       !value.empty? && value.end_with?("}")
209:     end
210:   end
211: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::OpenAI
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::OpenAI::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.unauthorized?
55:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
56:       elsif res.rate_limited?
57:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
58:       elsif res.not_found?
59:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
60:       else
61:         error = body["error"] || {}
62:         case error["type"]
63:         when "invalid_request_error" then handle_invalid_request(error)
64:         when "server_error"
65:           LLM::ServerError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
66:         else
67:           LLM::Error.new(error["message"] || "Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @param [Exception] error
74:     # @return [LLM::Error]
75:     def handle_invalid_request(error)
76:       case error["code"]
77:       when "context_length_exceeded"
78:         LLM::ContextWindowError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
79:       else
80:         LLM::InvalidRequestError.new(error["message"]).tap { _1.response = res }
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     ##
85:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
86:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
87:     def parse_body!

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The {LLM::Stream LLM::Stream} class provides the callback interface for
6:   # streamed model output in llm.rb.
7:   #
8:   # A stream object can be an instance of {LLM::Stream LLM::Stream} or a
9:   # subclass that overrides the callbacks it needs. For basic streaming,
10:   # llm.rb also accepts any object that implements `#<<`. {#queue} provides
11:   # a small helper for collecting asynchronous tool work started from a
12:   # callback.
13:   #
14:   # @note The `on_*` callbacks run inline with the streaming parser. They
15:   #   therefore block streaming progress and should generally return as
16:   #   quickly as possible.
17:   #
18:   # The most common callback is {#on_content}, which also maps to {#<<}.
19:   # Providers may also call {#on_reasoning_content} and {#on_tool_call} when
20:   # that data is available. Runtime features such as context compaction may
21:   # also emit lifecycle callbacks like {#on_transform} or {#on_compaction}.
22:   class Stream
23:     require_relative "stream/queue"
24: 
25:     ##
26:     # Returns extra context associated with the current streamed request.
27:     # @return [Hash]
28:     def extra
29:       @extra ||= LLM::Object.from({})
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Returns the current context, if one was attached to the stream.
34:     # @return [LLM::Context, nil]
35:     def ctx
36:       extra[:ctx]
37:     end
38: 
39:     ##
40:     # Returns a lazily-initialized queue for tool results or spawned work.
41:     # @return [LLM::Stream::Queue]
42:     def queue
43:       @queue ||= Queue.new(self)
44:     end
45: 
46:     ##
47:     # Waits for queued tool work to finish and returns function results.
48:     # Any passed arguments are ignored because queued work is waited according
49:     # to the actual task types already present in the queue.
50:     # @return [Array<LLM::Function::Return>]
51:     def wait(*)
52:       queue.wait
53:     end
54: 
55:     # @group Public callbacks
56: 
57:     ##
58:     # Called when visible assistant output is streamed.
59:     # @param [String] content
60:     #  A chunk of assistant-visible text.
61:     # @return [nil]
62:     def on_content(content)
63:       nil
64:     end
65:     alias_method :<<, :on_content
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # Called when reasoning output is streamed separately from visible content.
69:     # @param [String] content
70:     #  A chunk of reasoning text.
71:     # @return [nil]
72:     def on_reasoning_content(content)
73:       nil
74:     end
75: 
76:     ##
77:     # Called when a streamed tool call has been fully constructed.
78:     # @note A stream implementation may start tool execution here, for
79:     #   example by pushing `ctx.spawn(tool, :thread)`,
80:     #   `ctx.spawn(tool, :fiber)`, or `ctx.spawn(tool, :task)` onto {#queue}.
81:     #   Mixed strategies can also be selected per tool, such as
82:     #   `tool.mcp? ? ctx.spawn(tool, :task) : ctx.spawn(tool, :ractor)`.
83:     #   When a streamed tool cannot be resolved, `error` is passed as an
84:     #   {LLM::Function::Return}. It can be sent back to the model, allowing
85:     #   the tool-call path to recover and the session to continue. Streamed
86:     #   tool resolution now prefers the current request tools, so
87:     #   {LLM.function}, MCP tools, bound tool instances, and normal
88:     #   {LLM::Tool LLM::Tool} classes can all resolve through the same
89:     #   request-local path. The current `:ractor` mode is for class-based
90:     #   tools and does not support MCP tools.
91:     # @param [LLM::Function] tool
92:     #  The parsed tool call.
93:     # @param [LLM::Function::Return, nil] error
94:     #  An in-band tool error for unresolved tool calls.
95:     # @return [nil]
96:     def on_tool_call(tool, error)
97:       nil
98:     end
99: 
100:     ##
101:     # Called when queued streamed tool work returns.
102:     # @note This callback runs when {#wait} resolves work that was queued from
103:     #   {#on_tool_call}, such as values returned by `ctx.spawn(tool, :thread)`,
104:     #   `ctx.spawn(tool, :fiber)`, or `ctx.spawn(tool, :task)`.
105:     # @param [LLM::Function] tool
106:     #  The tool that returned.
107:     # @param [LLM::Function::Return] result
108:     #  The completed tool return.
109:     # @return [nil]
110:     def on_tool_return(tool, result)
111:       nil
112:     end
113: 
114:     ##
115:     # Called before a context transformer rewrites a prompt.
116:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
117:     # @param [#call] transformer
118:     # @return [nil]
119:     def on_transform(ctx, transformer)
120:       nil
121:     end
122: 
123:     ##
124:     # Called after a context transformer finishes rewriting a prompt.
125:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
126:     # @param [#call] transformer
127:     # @return [nil]
128:     def on_transform_finish(ctx, transformer)
129:       nil
130:     end
131: 
132:     ##
133:     # Called before a context compaction starts.
134:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
135:     # @param [LLM::Compactor] compactor
136:     # @return [nil]
137:     def on_compaction(ctx, compactor)
138:       nil
139:     end
140: 
141:     ##
142:     # Called after a context compaction finishes.
143:     # @param [LLM::Context] ctx
144:     # @param [LLM::Compactor] compactor
145:     # @return [nil]
146:     def on_compaction_finish(ctx, compactor)
147:       nil
148:     end
149: 
150:     # @endgroup
151: 
152:     # @group Finders
153: 
154:     ##
155:     # Resolves a streamed tool call against the current request tools first,
156:     # then falls back to the global function registry.
157:     # @param [String] name
158:     # @return [LLM::Function, nil]
159:     def __find__(name)
160:       tools = extra[:tools] || ctx&.params&.dig(:tools) || []
161:       tool = tools.find do
162:         candidate = _1.respond_to?(:function) ? _1.function.name : _1.name
163:         candidate.to_s == name.to_s
164:       end
165:       if tool
166:         tool.respond_to?(:function) ? tool.function : tool
167:       else
168:         LLM::Function.find_by_name(name)
169:       end
170:     end
171: 
172:     # @endgroup
173:   end
174: end

3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   module RequestAdapter
7:     require_relative "request_adapter/completion"
8: 
9:     ##
10:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
11:     #  The messages to adapt
12:     # @return [Hash]
13:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
14:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
15:       messages.each do |message|
16:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
17:         next if adapted.nil?
18:         if system?(message)
19:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
20:         else
21:           payload[:messages] << adapted
22:         end
23:       end
24:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
25:       payload
26:     end
27: 
28:     private
29: 
30:     ##
31:     # @param [Hash] params
32:     # @return [Hash]
33:     def adapt_tools(tools)
34:       return {} unless tools&.any?
35:       {tools: tools.map { _1.respond_to?(:adapt) ? _1.adapt(self) : _1 }}
36:     end
37: 
38:     def system?(message)
39:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
40:         message.system?
41:       else
42:         Hash === message and message[:role].to_s == "system"
43:       end
44:     end
45:   end

3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]

11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]

21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server

25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)

27:       @span = span

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Anthropic
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
20:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
21:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
22:     end
23: 
24:     ##
25:     # @param [Hash] chunk
26:     # @return [LLM::Anthropic::StreamParser]
27:     def parse!(chunk)
28:       tap { merge!(chunk) }
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
33:     # @return [void]
34:     def free
35:     end
36: 
37:     private
38: 
39:     def merge!(chunk)
40:       if chunk["type"] == "message_start"
41:         merge_message!(chunk["message"])
42:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_start"
43:         @body["content"][chunk["index"]] = chunk["content_block"]
44:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_delta"
45:         if chunk["delta"]["type"] == "text_delta"
46:           @body["content"][chunk["index"]]["text"] << chunk["delta"]["text"]
47:           emit_content(chunk["delta"]["text"])
48:         elsif chunk["delta"]["type"] == "input_json_delta"
49:           content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
50:           if Hash === content["input"]
51:             content["input"] = chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
52:           else
53:             content["input"] ||= +""
54:             content["input"] << chunk["delta"]["partial_json"]
55:           end
56:         end
57:       elsif chunk["type"] == "message_delta"
58:         merge_message!(chunk["delta"]) if chunk["delta"]
59:         extras = chunk.reject { |k, _| k == "type" || k == "delta" }
60:         merge_message!(extras) unless extras.empty?
61:       elsif chunk["type"] == "content_block_stop"
62:         content = @body["content"][chunk["index"]]
63:         if content["input"]
64:           content["input"] = LLM.json.load(content["input"])
65:         end
66:         if content["type"] == "tool_use"
67:           emit_tool(content)
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_message!(message)
73:       message.each_pair do |key, value|
74:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair)
75:           @body[key] ||= {}
76:           deep_merge!(@body[key], value)
77:         else
78:           @body[key] = value
79:         end
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def deep_merge!(target, source)
84:       source.each_pair do |key, value|
85:         if value.respond_to?(:each_pair) && target[key].respond_to?(:each_pair)
86:           deep_merge!(target[key], value)
87:         else
88:           target[key] = value
89:         end
90:       end
91:     end
92: 
93:     def emit_content(value)
94:       if @can_emit_content
95:         @stream.on_content(value)
96:       elsif @can_push_content
97:         @stream << value
98:       end
99:     end
100: 
101:     def emit_tool(tool)
102:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
103:       function, error = resolve_tool(tool)
104:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
105:     end
106: 
107:     def resolve_tool(tool)
108:       registered = @stream.__find__(tool["name"])
109:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(tool["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
110:         fn.id = tool["id"]
111:         fn.arguments = LLM::Anthropic.parse_tool_input(tool["input"])
112:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
113:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
114:       end
115:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
116:     end
117:   end
118: end

47:     # @raise (see LLM::Provider#request)

49:     def embed(input, model: default_model, **params)
50:       params   = {model:}.merge!(params)
51:       req      = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/v1/embeddings", headers)
52:       req.body = LLM.json.dump({input:}.merge!(params))
53:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, operation: "embeddings", model:)
54:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :embedding)
55:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "embeddings", model:, res:, span:)

73:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
74:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
75:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
76:       res
77:     end

80:     # Provides an interface to Ollama's models API
81:     # @see https://github.com/ollama/ollama/blob/main/docs/api.md#list-local-models Ollama docs
82:     # @return [LLM::Ollama::Models]
83:     def models
84:       LLM::Ollama::Models.new(self)
85:     end
86:

120:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
121:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, stream: true}.merge!(params)
122:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
123:       params = [params, {format: params[:schema]}, adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
124:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
125:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
126:       [params, stream, tools, role]

130:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
131:       body = LLM.json.dump({messages: [adapt(messages)].flatten}.merge!(params))
132:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post("/api/chat", headers)
133:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))

140:       else
141:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), LLM::Message.new(role, prompt)]
142:       end
143:     end

198:       tools  = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
199:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
200:       role, model, stream = [:role, :model, :stream].map { params.delete(_1) }

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class ErrorHandler
7:     ##
8:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
9:     #  Non-2XX response from the server
10:     attr_reader :res
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @return [Object, nil]
14:     #  The span
15:     attr_reader :span
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
19:     #  The tracer
20:     # @param [Object, nil] span
21:     #  The span
22:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
23:     #  The response from the server
24:     # @return [LLM::Google::ErrorHandler]
25:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
26:       @tracer = tracer
27:       @span = span
28:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
29:     end
30: 
31:     ##
32:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
33:     #  Raises a subclass of {LLM::Error LLM::Error}
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
45:     def body
46:       @body ||= parse_body!
47:     end
48: 
49:     ##
50:     # @return [LLM::Error]
51:     def error
52:       if res.server_error?
53:         LLM::ServerError.new("Server error").tap { _1.response = res }
54:       elsif res.bad_request?
55:         reason = body.dig("error", "details", 0, "reason")
56:         if reason == "API_KEY_INVALID"
57:           LLM::UnauthorizedError.new("Authentication error").tap { _1.response = res }
58:         else
59:           LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
60:         end
61:       elsif res.rate_limited?
62:         LLM::RateLimitError.new("Too many requests").tap { _1.response = res }
63:       elsif res.not_found?
64:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
65:       else
66:         LLM::Error.new("Unexpected response").tap { _1.response = res }
67:       end
68:     end
69: 
70:     ##
71:     # Tries to parse the response body as a LLM::Object
72:     # @return [String, LLM::Object]
73:     def parse_body!
74:       if String === res.body
75:         LLM::Object.from LLM.json.load(res.body)
76:       elsif Hash === res.body
77:         LLM::Object.from(res.body)
78:       else
79:         res.body
80:       end
81:     rescue
82:       res.body
83:     end
84:   end
85: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Google
4:   ##
5:   # @private
6:   class StreamParser
7:     ##
8:     # Returns the fully constructed response body
9:     # @return [Hash]
10:     attr_reader :body
11: 
12:     ##
13:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
14:     #  A stream sink that implements {#<<} or the {LLM::Stream} interface
15:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
16:     def initialize(stream)
17:       @body = {"candidates" => []}
18:       @stream = stream
19:       @emits = {tools: []}
20:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
21:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
22:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
23:     end
24: 
25:     ##
26:     # @param [Hash] chunk
27:     # @return [LLM::Google::StreamParser]
28:     def parse!(chunk)
29:       tap { merge_chunk!(chunk) }
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # Frees internal parser state used during streaming.
34:     # @return [void]
35:     def free
36:       @emits.clear
37:     end
38: 
39:     private
40: 
41:     def merge_chunk!(chunk)
42:       chunk.each do |key, value|
43:         k = key.to_s
44:         if k == "candidates"
45:           merge_candidates!(value)
46:         elsif k == "usageMetadata" &&
47:             @body["usageMetadata"].is_a?(Hash) &&
48:             value.is_a?(Hash)
49:           @body["usageMetadata"] = @body["usageMetadata"].merge(value)
50:         else
51:           @body[k] = value
52:         end
53:       end
54:     end
55: 
56:     def merge_candidates!(deltas)
57:       deltas.each do |delta|
58:         index = delta["index"].to_i
59:         @body["candidates"][index] ||= {"content" => {"parts" => []}}
60:         candidate = @body["candidates"][index]
61:         delta.each do |key, value|
62:           k = key.to_s
63:           if k == "content"
64:             merge_candidate_content!(candidate["content"], value, index) if value
65:           else
66:             candidate[k] = value # Overwrite other fields
67:           end
68:         end
69:       end
70:     end
71: 
72:     def merge_candidate_content!(content, delta, cindex)
73:       delta.each do |key, value|
74:         k = key.to_s
75:         if k == "parts"
76:           content["parts"] ||= []
77:           merge_content_parts!(content["parts"], value, cindex) if value
78:         else
79:           content[k] = value
80:         end
81:       end
82:     end
83: 
84:     def merge_content_parts!(parts, deltas, cindex)
85:       deltas.each do |delta|
86:         if delta["text"]
87:           merge_text!(parts, delta)
88:         elsif delta["functionCall"]
89:           merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
90:         elsif delta["inlineData"]
91:           parts << delta
92:         elsif delta["functionResponse"]
93:           parts << delta
94:         elsif delta["fileData"]
95:           parts << delta
96:         else
97:           parts << delta
98:         end
99:       end
100:     end
101: 
102:     def merge_text!(parts, delta)
103:       last_existing_part = parts.last
104:       text = delta["text"]
105:       if last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) && last_existing_part["text"]
106:         last_existing_part["text"] ||= +""
107:         last_existing_part["text"] << text
108:         emit_content(text)
109:       else
110:         parts << delta
111:         emit_content(text)
112:       end
113:     end
114: 
115:     def merge_function_call!(parts, delta, cindex)
116:       last_existing_part = parts.last
117:       last_call = last_existing_part.is_a?(Hash) ? last_existing_part["functionCall"] : nil
118:       delta_call = delta["functionCall"]
119:       if last_call.is_a?(Hash) && delta_call.is_a?(Hash)
120:         last_existing_part["functionCall"] = last_call.merge(delta_call)
121:         delta.each do |key, value|
122:           next if key == "functionCall"
123:           last_existing_part[key] = value
124:         end
125:       else
126:         parts << delta
127:       end
128:       emit_tool(parts.length - 1, cindex, parts.last || delta)
129:     end
130: 
131:     def emit_content(value)
132:       if @can_emit_content
133:         @stream.on_content(value)
134:       elsif @can_push_content
135:         @stream << value
136:       end
137:     end
138: 
139:     def emit_tool(pindex, cindex, part)
140:       return unless @can_emit_tool_call
141:       return unless complete_tool?(part)
142:       key = [cindex, pindex]
143:       return if @emits[:tools].include?(key)
144:       function, error = resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
145:       @emits[:tools] << key
146:       @stream.on_tool_call(function, error)
147:     end
148: 
149:     def complete_tool?(part)
150:       call = part["functionCall"]
151:       call && call["name"] && Hash === call["args"]
152:     end
153: 
154:     def resolve_tool(part, cindex, pindex)
155:       call = part["functionCall"]
156:       registered = @stream.__find__(call["name"])
157:       fn = (registered || LLM::Function.new(call["name"])).dup.tap do |fn|
158:         fn.id = LLM::Google.tool_id(part:, cindex:, pindex:)
159:         fn.arguments = call["args"]
160:         fn.tracer = @stream.extra[:tracer]
161:         fn.model = @stream.extra[:model]
162:       end
163:       [fn, (registered ? nil : fn.unavailable)]
164:     end
165:   end
166: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Transport
4:   ##
5:   # Internal request execution methods for {LLM::Provider}.
6:   #
7:   # This module handles provider-side transport execution, response
8:   # parsing, streaming, and request body setup.
9:   #
10:   # @api private
11:   module Execution
12:     private
13: 
14:     ##
15:     # Executes a HTTP request
16:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Request] request
17:     #  The request to send
18:     # @param [Proc] b
19:     #  A block to yield the response to (optional)
20:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
21:     #  The response from the server
22:     # @raise [LLM::Error::Unauthorized]
23:     #  When authentication fails
24:     # @raise [LLM::Error::RateLimit]
25:     #  When the rate limit is exceeded
26:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
27:     #  When any other unsuccessful status code is returned
28:     # @raise [SystemCallError]
29:     #  When there is a network error at the operating system level
30:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
31:     def execute(request:, operation:, stream: nil, stream_parser: self.stream_parser, model: nil, inputs: nil, &b)
32:       stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
33:       owner = transport.request_owner
34:       tracer = self.tracer
35:       span = tracer.on_request_start(operation:, model:, inputs:)
36:       res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
37:       res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
38:       [handle_response(res, tracer, span), span, tracer]
39:     rescue *transport.interrupt_errors
40:       raise LLM::Interrupt, "request interrupted" if transport.interrupted?(owner)
41:       raise
42:     end
43: 
44:     ##
45:     # Handles the response from a request
46:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
47:     #  The response to handle
48:     # @param [Object, nil] span
49:     #  The span
50:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
51:     def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
52:       res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
53:       res
54:     end
55: 
56:     ##
57:     # Parse a HTTP response
58:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response] res
59:     # @return [LLM::Object, String]
60:     def parse_response(res)
61:       case res["content-type"]
62:       when %r{\Aapplication/json\s*} then LLM::Object.from(LLM.json.load(res.body))
63:       else res.body
64:       end
65:     end
66:   end
67: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: module LLM
4:   ##
5:   # The Bedrock class implements a provider for
6:   # [Amazon Bedrock](https://aws.amazon.com/bedrock/).
7:   #
8:   # Bedrock provides access to foundation models from Anthropic, Meta,
9:   # Mistral, AI21 Labs, Cohere, and more through the AWS infrastructure.
10:   # This provider uses the Bedrock Converse API for chat completions,
11:   # and the Converse Stream API for streaming.
12:   #
13:   # Unlike other llm.rb providers which use API key authentication,
14:   # Bedrock uses AWS Signature V4 (SigV4) for request signing.
15:   # You must provide AWS credentials (access key, secret key, and region)
16:   # instead of a single API key.
17:   #
18:   # Streaming uses the AWS Event Stream binary protocol instead of
19:   # standard SSE. The binary framing is decoded inline using only
20:   # Ruby's stdlib.
21:   #
22:   # @example
23:   #   require "llm"
24:   #
25:   #   llm = LLM.bedrock(
26:   #     access_key_id: ENV["AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID"],
27:   #     secret_access_key: ENV["AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY"],
28:   #     region: "us-east-1"
29:   #   )
30:   #   ctx = LLM::Context.new(llm)
31:   #   ctx.talk "Hello, how are you?"
32:   #   ctx.messages.select(&:assistant?).each { puts _1.content }
33:   class Bedrock < Provider
34:     require_relative "bedrock/signature"
35:     require_relative "bedrock/error_handler"
36:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter"
37:     require_relative "bedrock/request_adapter/completion"

51:     # @param [String] secret_access_key AWS secret access key
52:     # @param [String] region AWS region (e.g. "us-east-1")
53:     # @param [String, nil] session_token AWS session token for temporary credentials
54:     # @param [String, nil] host Override the Bedrock API host
55:     # @param [Integer] port Connection port
56:     # @param [Boolean] ssl Whether to use SSL
57:     # @param [Integer] timeout Request timeout in seconds
58:     def initialize(access_key_id: nil, secret_access_key: nil,
59:                    region: nil, session_token: nil,
60:                    host: nil, port: 443, ssl: true, timeout: 60,
61:                    **)
62:       region ||= "us-east-1"
63:       @access_key_id = access_key_id
64:       @secret_access_key = secret_access_key
65:       @aws_region = region
66:       @session_token = session_token
67:       host ||= HOST_PATTERN % region
68:       @aws_host = host
69:       super(key: @access_key_id, host:, port:, ssl:, timeout:, persistent: false)
70:     end
71: 
72:     ##
73:     # @return [Symbol] Returns the provider's name
74:     def name
75:       :bedrock
76:     end
77: 
78:     ##
79:     # Provides an interface to the Bedrock Converse API
80:     #
81:     # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_runtime_Converse.html
82:     #
83:     # @param prompt (see LLM::Provider#complete)
84:     # @param params (see LLM::Provider#complete)
85:     # @return (see LLM::Provider#complete)
86:     def complete(prompt, params = {})
87:       params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
88:       req, messages, body = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream:)
89:       tracer.set_request_metadata(user_input: extract_user_input(messages, fallback: prompt))
90:       sign!(req, body)
91:       model_id = model_id_for(req.path)
92:       res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream:, operation: "chat", stream_parser:, model: model_id)
93:       res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
94:         .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
95:       tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: model_id, res:, span:)
96:       res
97:     end
98: 
99:     ##
100:     # Provides an interface to Bedrock's ListFoundationModels API.
101:     #
102:     # @note
103:     #  Unlike the Converse API (bedrock-runtime), this endpoint lives
104:     #  on the control plane (bedrock.<region>.amazonaws.com).
105:     #
106:     # @see https://docs.aws.amazon.com/bedrock/latest/APIReference/API_ListFoundationModels.html
107:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::Models]
108:     def models
109:       LLM::Bedrock::Models.new(self)
110:     end
111:

114:     def files
115:       raise NotImplementedError
116:     end

150:     def embed(input, model: nil, **params)

154:     ##

162:     # `toolResult` content blocks rather than a distinct `tool` role.

174:     private

176:     def headers
177:       lock do
178:         (@headers || {}).merge("Content-Type" => "application/json")
179:       end
180:     end
181: 
182:     def credentials
183:       LLM::Object.from(
184:         access_key_id: @access_key_id,
185:         secret_access_key: @secret_access_key,
186:         aws_region: @aws_region,
187:         host: @aws_host,
188:         session_token: @session_token
189:       )
190:     end
191: 
192:     def stream_parser
193:       LLM::Bedrock::StreamParser
194:     end
195: 
196:     def error_handler
197:       LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler
198:     end
199: 
200:     def stream_decoder
201:       LLM::Bedrock::StreamDecoder
202:     end
203: 
204:     def normalize_complete_params(params)
205:       params = {role: :user, model: default_model, max_tokens: 2048}.merge!(params)
206:       tools = resolve_tools(params.delete(:tools))
207:       params = [params, adapt_schema(params), adapt_tools(tools)].inject({}, &:merge!).compact
208:       role, stream = params.delete(:role), params.delete(:stream)
209:       params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
210:       [params, stream, tools, role]
211:     end
212: 
213:     def build_complete_request(prompt, params, role, stream: nil)
214:       messages = build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
215:       model_id = params.delete(:model) || default_model
216:       payload = build_converse_payload(messages, params)
217:       body = LLM.json.dump(payload)
218:       path = stream ? "/model/#{model_id}/converse-stream" \
219:                     : "/model/#{model_id}/converse"
220:       req = LLM::Transport::Request.post(path, headers)
221:       transport.set_body_stream(req, StringIO.new(body))
222:       [req, messages, body]
223:     end
224: 
225:     def build_complete_messages(prompt, params, role)
226:       if LLM::Prompt === prompt
227:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), *prompt]
228:       else
229:         [*(params.delete(:messages) || []), Message.new(role, prompt)]
230:       end
231:     end
232: 
233:     def build_converse_payload(messages, params)
234:       adapted = adapt(messages)
235:       payload = {}
236:       payload[:system] = adapted[:system] if adapted[:system]&.any?
237:       payload[:messages] = adapted[:messages]
238:       inference_config = {}
239:       inference_config[:maxTokens] = params.delete(:max_tokens) if params[:max_tokens]
240:       inference_config[:temperature] = params.delete(:temperature) if params.key?(:temperature)
241:       inference_config[:topP] = params.delete(:top_p) if params.key?(:top_p)
242:       inference_config[:stopSequences] = params.delete(:stop) if params[:stop]
243:       payload[:inferenceConfig] = inference_config unless inference_config.empty?
244:       payload[:toolConfig] = params.delete(:toolConfig) if params[:toolConfig]
245:       payload[:outputConfig] = params.delete(:outputConfig) if params[:outputConfig]
246:       additional = {}
247:       top_k = params.delete(:top_k)
248:       additional[:top_k] = top_k if top_k
249:       payload[:additionalModelRequestFields] = additional unless additional.empty?
250:       payload
251:     end
252: 
253:     def extract_user_input(messages, fallback:)
254:       message = messages.reverse.find(&:user?) || messages.last
255:       value = message&.content || fallback
256:       value.is_a?(String) ? value : LLM.json.dump(value)
257:     end
258: 
259:     def model_id_for(path)
260:       path[%r{\A/model/(.+?)/converse(?:-stream)?\z}, 1] || default_model
261:     end
262: 
263:     def sign!(req, body)
264:       Signature.new(
265:         credentials:,
266:         method: req.method,
267:         path: req.path,
268:         body:
269:       ).sign!(req)

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Adapts llm.rb internal message format to Bedrock Converse API format.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock Converse uses:
8:   #   - system: [{text: "..."}]  (top-level, separate from messages)
9:   #   - messages: [{role: "user"|"assistant", content: [{...}, ...]}]
10:   #   - Content blocks: text, image, document, toolUse, toolResult
11:   #   - toolConfig: {tools: [{toolSpec: {name:, description:, inputSchema: {json: ...}}}]}
12:   #
13:   # @api private
14:   module RequestAdapter
15:     ##
16:     # @param [Array<LLM::Message>] messages
17:     # @return [Hash]
18:     def adapt(messages, mode: nil)
19:       payload = {messages: [], system: []}
20:       messages.each do |message|
21:         adapted = Completion.new(message).adapt
22:         next if adapted.nil?
23:         if system?(message)
24:           payload[:system].concat Array(adapted[:content])
25:         else
26:           payload[:messages] << adapted
27:         end
28:       end
29:       payload.delete(:system) if payload[:system].empty?
30:       payload
31:     end
32: 
33:     private
34: 
35:     ##
36:     # @param [Hash] params
37:     # @return [Hash]
38:     def adapt_schema(params)
39:       return {} unless params&.key?(:schema)
40:       schema = params.delete(:schema)
41:       schema = schema.respond_to?(:object) ? schema.object : schema
42:       cleaned = schema.respond_to?(:to_h) ? schema.to_h : schema
43:       [:strict, "strict", :$schema, "$schema"].each { cleaned.delete(_1) }
44:       {
45:         outputConfig: {
46:           textFormat: {
47:             type: "json_schema",
48:             structure: {
49:               jsonSchema: {
50:                 name: "response",
51:                 schema: LLM.json.dump(cleaned)
52:               }
53:             }
54:           }
55:         }
56:       }
57:     end
58: 
59:     ##
60:     # @param [Array<LLM::Function>] tools
61:     # @return [Hash]
62:     def adapt_tools(tools)
63:       return {} unless tools&.any?
64:       {toolConfig: {tools: tools.map { |t| adapt_tool(t) }}}
65:     end
66: 
67:     ##
68:     # @param [LLM::Function] tool
69:     # @return [Hash]
70:     def adapt_tool(tool)
71:       function = tool.respond_to?(:function) ? tool.function : tool
72:       {
73:         toolSpec: {
74:           name: function.name,
75:           description: function.description,
76:           inputSchema: {
77:             json: function.params || default_input_schema
78:           }
79:         }
80:       }
81:     end
82: 
83:     def default_input_schema
84:       {"type" => "object", "properties" => {}, "required" => []}
85:     end
86: 
87:     def system?(message)
88:       if message.respond_to?(:system?)
89:         message.system?
90:       else
91:         Hash === message && message[:role].to_s == "system"
92:       end
93:     end
94:   end
95: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true

3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Adapts Bedrock Converse API responses to llm.rb's Response format.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock Converse returns:
8:   #   {
9:   #     output: {message: {role: "assistant", content: [{...}, ...]}},
10:   #     usage: {inputTokens: N, outputTokens: N},
11:   #     modelId: "anthropic.claude-...",
12:   #     stopReason: "end_turn" | "tool_use" | "max_tokens" | ...
13:   #   }
14:   #
15:   # @api private
16:   module ResponseAdapter
17:     module_function
18: 
19:     ##
20:     # @param [LLM::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
21:     # @param [Symbol] type
22:     # @return [LLM::Response]
23:     def adapt(res, type:)
24:       response = (LLM::Response === res) ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
25:       response.extend(select(type))
26:     end
27: 
28:     ##
29:     # @api private
30:     def select(type)
31:       case type
32:       when :completion then LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter::Completion
33:       when :models then LLM::Bedrock::ResponseAdapter::Models
34:       else
35:         raise ArgumentError,
36:               "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
37:       end
38:     end
39:   end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Handles Bedrock API error responses.
6:   #
7:   # Bedrock errors come as JSON with:
8:   #   { "message" => "...", "__type" => "..." }
9:   # or as standard HTTP status codes.
10:   #
11:   # @api private
12:   class ErrorHandler
13:     ##
14:     # @return [LLM::Transport::Response]
15:     attr_reader :res
16: 
17:     ##
18:     # @return [Object, nil]
19:     attr_reader :span
20: 
21:     ##
22:     # @param [LLM::Tracer] tracer
23:     # @param [Object, nil] span
24:     # @param [LLM::Transport::Response, Net::HTTPResponse] res
25:     # @return [LLM::Bedrock::ErrorHandler]
26:     def initialize(tracer, span, res)
27:       @tracer = tracer
28:       @span = span
29:       @res = LLM::Transport::Response.from(res)
30:     end
31: 
32:     ##
33:     # @raise [LLM::Error]
34:     def raise_error!
35:       ex = error
36:       @tracer.on_request_error(ex:, span:)
37:     ensure
38:       raise(ex) if ex
39:     end
40: 
41:     private
42: 
43:     ##
44:     # @return [LLM::Error]
45:     def error
46:       message = extract_message
47:       if res.server_error?
48:         LLM::ServerError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
49:       elsif res.unauthorized?
50:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
51:       elsif res.forbidden?
52:         LLM::UnauthorizedError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
53:       elsif res.rate_limited?
54:         LLM::RateLimitError.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
55:       elsif res.not_found?
56:         LLM::NotFoundError.new("Server response: not found (404)").tap { _1.response = res }
57:       else
58:         LLM::Error.new(message).tap { _1.response = res }
59:       end
60:     end
61: 
62:     ##
63:     # @return [String]
64:     def extract_message
65:       body = parse_body
66:       body["message"] || body["Message"] || body["__type"] || "Unexpected error"
67:     end
68: 
69:     ##
70:     # @return [Hash]
71:     def parse_body
72:       return {} if res.body.nil? || res.body.empty?
73:       parsed = LLM.json.load(res.body.dup.force_encoding(Encoding::UTF_8).scrub)
74:       Hash === parsed ? parsed : {}
75:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
76:       {}
77:     end
78:   end
79: end

1: # frozen_string_literal: true
2: 
3: class LLM::Bedrock
4:   ##
5:   # Parses Bedrock Converse Stream events into a response body
6:   # and emits stream callbacks (on_content, on_tool_call, etc.).
7:   #
8:   # Receives decoded JSON payloads from {StreamDecoder} along with
9:   # the AWS Event Stream event type header.
10:   #
11:   # Bedrock Converse Stream event types:
12:   #   messageStart — initial role
13:   #   contentBlockStart — tool use or reasoning start
14:   #   contentBlockDelta — text delta, tool input JSON, or reasoning text
15:   #   contentBlockStop — content block finished
16:   #   messageStop — final stop reason, usage metadata
17:   #
18:   # @api private
19:   class StreamParser
20:     TOOL_MARKER = "<｜DSML｜function_calls"
21: 
22:     ##
23:     # @return [Hash] Fully constructed response body
24:     attr_reader :body
25: 
26:     ##
27:     # @param [#<<, LLM::Stream] stream
28:     def initialize(stream)
29:       @body = {"output" => {"message" => {"role" => "assistant", "content" => []}}}
30:       @stream = stream
31:       @text_markers = {}
32:       @can_emit_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_content)
33:       @can_emit_reasoning_content = stream.respond_to?(:on_reasoning_content)
34:       @can_emit_tool_call = stream.respond_to?(:on_tool_call)
35:       @can_push_content = stream.respond_to?(:<<)
36:     end
37: 
38:     ##
39:     # @param [Hash] payload Decoded JSON from an event stream frame
40:     # @param [String, nil] event_type The :event-type header value
41:     # @return [self]
42:     def parse!(payload, event_type: nil)
43:       type = event_type || payload["type"]
44:       case type
45:       when "messageStart"
46:         # { "role" => "assistant" }
47:       when "contentBlockStart"
48:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "start" => { "toolUse" => {...} } }
49:         handle_content_block_start(payload)
50:       when "contentBlockDelta"
51:         # { "contentBlockIndex" => 0, "delta" => { "text" => "..." } }
52:         handle_content_block_delta(payload)
53:       when "contentBlockStop"
54:         handle_content_block_stop(payload)
55:       when "messageStop"
56:         # { "stopReason" => "end_turn", "metadata" => {"usage" => {...}} }
57:         merge_metadata(payload)
58:       when "metadata"
59:         # { "usage" => {...} }
60:         merge_metadata(payload)
61:       end
62:       self
63:     end
64: 
65:     ##
66:     # @return [void]
67:     def free
68:       @text_markers.clear
69:     end
70: 
71:     private
72: 
73:     def handle_content_block_start(payload)
74:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
75:       start_data = payload["start"] || {}
76:       if (tool_use = start_data["toolUse"])
77:         content[index] = {"toolUse" => {"toolUseId" => tool_use["toolUseId"], "name" => tool_use["name"], "input" => +""}}
78:       elsif (reasoning = start_data["reasoningContent"])
79:         content[index] = {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +"", "signature" => reasoning["signature"]}.compact}
80:       end
81:     end
82: 
83:     def handle_content_block_delta(payload)
84:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
85:       delta = payload["delta"] || {}
86:       if (text = delta["text"])
87:         ensure_content_block(index, "text")
88:         visible = filtered_text(index, text)
89:         return if visible.empty?
90:         content[index]["text"] ||= +""
91:         content[index]["text"] << visible
92:         emit_content(visible)
93:       elsif (tool_input = delta.dig("toolUse", "input"))
94:         ensure_content_block(index, "tool_use")
95:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] ||= +""
96:         content[index]["toolUse"]["input"] << tool_input
97:       elsif (reasoning = delta["reasoningContent"])
98:         ensure_content_block(index, "reasoning")
99:         if reasoning["text"]
100:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] ||= +""
101:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["text"] << reasoning["text"]
102:           emit_reasoning_content(reasoning["text"])
103:         end
104:         if reasoning["signature"]
105:           content[index]["reasoningContent"]["signature"] = reasoning["signature"]
106:         end
107:       end
108:     end
109: 
110:     def handle_content_block_stop(payload)
111:       index = payload["contentBlockIndex"]
112:       item = content[index]
113:       return unless item
114:       flush_text(index, item)
115:       if item["toolUse"] && item["toolUse"]["input"].is_a?(String)
116:         parsed = LLM.json.load(item["toolUse"]["input"])
117:         item["toolUse"]["input"] = parsed.is_a?(Hash) ? parsed : {}
118:         emit_tool(item)
119:       end
120:     rescue *LLM.json.parser_error
121:       item["toolUse"]["input"] = {} if item&.dig("toolUse")
122:     end
123: 
124:     def ensure_content_block(index, type)
125:       content[index] ||= case type
126:       when "tool_use" then {"toolUse" => {"input" => +""}}
127:       when "reasoning" then {"reasoningContent" => {"text" => +""}}
128:       else {}
129:       end
130:     end
131: 
132:     def filtered_text(index, text)
133:       state = (@text_markers[index] ||= +"")
134:       value = state << text
135:       value.gsub!(TOOL_MARKER, "")
136:       keep = marker_prefix_length(value)
137:       @text_markers[index] = keep.zero? ? +"" : value[-keep..]
138:       keep.zero? ? value : value[0...-keep]
139:     end
140: 
141:     def flush_text(index, item)
142:       value = @text_markers.delete(index).to_s
143:       return unless item["text"]
144:       if value.empty?
145:         content[index] = {} if item["text"].empty?
146:       else
147:         item["text"] << value
148:         emit_content(value)
149:       end
150:     end
151: 
152:     def marker_prefix_length(value)
153:       [value.length, TOOL_MARKER.length - 1].min.downto(1) do |length|
154:         return length if TOOL_MARKER.start_with?(value[-length..])
155:       end
156:       0
157:     end
158: 
159:     def merge_metadata(payload)
160:       metadata = payload["metadata"] || payload
161:       return unless metadata.is_a?(Hash)
162:       usage = metadata["usage"]
163:       @body["usage"] = usage if usage
164:       @body["stopReason"] = payload["stopReason"] if payload["stopReason"]
165:     end
166: 
167:     def emit_content(value)
168:       if @can_emit_content
169:         @stream.on_content(value)
170:       elsif @can_push_content
171:         @stream << value
172:       end
173:     end
174: 
175:     def emit_reasoning_content(value)
176:       @stream.on_reasoning_content(value) if @can_emit_reasoning_content
177:     end
178: 
179:     def emit_tool(tool)

## Complete llm.rb Codebase Structure Analysis

### 1. **Top-Level Provider Files** (`lib/llm/providers/`)

**Eight primary providers:**
- `openai.rb` - OpenAI provider
- `anthropic.rb` - Anthropic provider
- `bedrock.rb` - Amazon Bedrock provider
- `ollama.rb` - Ollama provider
- `google.rb` - Google Gemini provider
- `deepseek.rb` - DeepSeek provider (extends OpenAI)
- `xai.rb` - xAI provider (extends OpenAI)
- `zai.rb` - zAI provider (extends OpenAI)
- `llamacpp.rb` - Llama.cpp provider (extends OpenAI)

### 2. **Provider Registry/Data JSON Files** (`data/`)

**Seven JSON configuration files:**
- `data/openai.json`
- `data/anthropic.json`
- `data/bedrock.json`
- `data/ollama.json` (implied from file structure)
- `data/google.json`
- `data/deepseek.json`
- `data/xai.json`
- `data/zai.json`

### 3. **Provider Spec Files** (Tests)

**Provider-specific test files:**
- `spec/provider_spec.rb` - Core Provider tests (OpenAI, Bedrock)
- `spec/openai/*_spec.rb` - 16 OpenAI tests
- `spec/anthropic/*_spec.rb` - 6 Anthropic tests
- `spec/bedrock/*_spec.rb` - 5 Bedrock tests
- `spec/ollama/*_spec.rb` - 3 Ollama tests
- `spec/google/*_spec.rb` - 7 Google tests
- `spec/deepseek/*_spec.rb` - 3 DeepSeek tests
- `spec/xai/*_spec.rb` - 3 xAI tests

---

## Inheritance & Shared Module Relationships

### **Class Hierarchy**

```
LLM::Provider (abstract base class)
├── LLM::OpenAI
│   ├── LLM::DeepSeek (extends OpenAI)
│   ├── LLM::XAI (extends OpenAI)
│   ├── LLM::ZAI (extends OpenAI)
│   └── LLM::LlamaCpp (extends OpenAI)
├── LLM::Anthropic
├── LLM::Bedrock
├── LLM::Ollama
└── LLM::Google
```

### **Shared Module Architecture**

**Each provider has a consistent module structure:**

#### **RequestAdapter Module** (`lib/llm/providers/<provider>/request_adapter.rb`)
- Adapts internal `LLM::Message` format to provider's API format
- Contains `adapt(messages)` method
- Provider-specific submodules:
  - `Completion` - for chat completions
  - `Respond` - for responses API (OpenAI)
  - `Moderation` - for moderation API (OpenAI)

#### **ResponseAdapter Module** (`lib/llm/providers/<provider>/response_adapter.rb`)
- Adapts provider API responses to `LLM::Response` format
- Contains `adapt(res, type:)` method with dispatch logic
- Type-specific adapters:
  - `Completion` - chat completions
  - `Embedding` - embeddings
  - `File` / `Files` - file operations
  - `Image` / `Images` - image operations
  - `Audio` - audio operations
  - `Moderations` - moderation results
  - `Models` - model listings
  - `WebSearch` - web search results
  - `Enumerable` - collection operations
  - `Responds` - responses API (OpenAI)

#### **Supporting Modules per Provider**

**OpenAI:**
- `ErrorHandler`, `StreamParser`, `Models`, `Images`, `Audio`, `Files`, `Moderations`, `VectorStores`, `Responses`

**Anthropic:**
- `ErrorHandler`, `StreamParser`, `Models`, `Files`, `Utils` (module with helpers)

**Bedrock:**
- `ErrorHandler`, `StreamParser`, `StreamDecoder`, `Models`, `Signature` (AWS SigV4 signing)

**Ollama:**
- `ErrorHandler`, `StreamParser`, `Models`

**Google:**
- `ErrorHandler`, `StreamParser`, `Models`, `Images`, `Audio`, `Files`, `Utils`

**DeepSeek/XAI/ZAI/LlamaCpp:**
- Reuse OpenAI modules via `require_relative "openai/..."` and `include RequestAdapter`

---

## Key Relationship Patterns

1. **Inheritance**: DeepSeek, XAI, ZAI, and LlamaCpp inherit from OpenAI
2. **Module Inclusion**: All providers `include RequestAdapter` module
3. **Extends**: Some providers (Anthropic, Google) use `extend Utils`
4. **Shared Code**:OpenAI adapters are shared via `require_relative` by derivative providers
5. **Composition**: Each provider uses specialized modules for different responsibilities (request, response, error handling, streaming)

## Provider Method Coverage

**Full-featured providers** (OpenAI, Anthropic, Google):
- `complete()`, `embed()`, `chat()`, `respond()`
- `files()`, `images()`, `audio()`, `moderations()`, `responses()`, `vector_stores()`, `models()`
- `web_search()`, `server_tools()`

**Reduced providers** (DeepSeek, XAI, ZAI, LlamaCpp, Ollama, Bedrock):
- Varies in feature support; many raise `NotImplementedError` for advanced features
- All implement `complete()` and `models()`

Here's the complete map and analysis of the llm.rb codebase for adding a new LLM provider:

**Project Structure:**
- **Type:** Ruby library for LLM provider integrations
- **Organization:** Modular provider system with shared base class
- **Provider Location:** `lib/llm/providers/`
- **Shared Machinery:** `lib/llm/provider.rb` (abstract base) + transport/execution system
- **How callers obtain providers:** Module methods in `lib/llm.rb` (e.g., `LLM.openai(key:)`, `LLM.anthropic(key:)`)

**Key Files:**
- `lib/llm.rb` - Top-level library entry point with convenience methods
- `lib/llm/provider.rb` - Abstract base class all providers inherit from (lines 1-431)
- `lib/llm/registry.rb` - Model registry for pricing/capabilities (lines 1-81)
- `lib/llm/error.rb` - Shared error types (lines 1-89)
- `lib/llm/response.rb` - Normalized response format (lines 1-84)
- `lib/llm/stream.rb` - Streaming callback interface (lines 1-174)
- `lib/llm/transport/execution.rb` - Request execution (lines 1-67)
- `lib/llm/contract/completion.rb` - Response interface contract (lines 1-131)
- `data/` - JSON registry files for each provider's models
- `spec/` - Test files per provider

---

### **Step 2: Trace a Chat Call**

**Order of execution for `llm.complete(prompt, params)` (lines 1-97 in provider.rb, 73-82 in openai.rb):**

1. **Caller obtains provider** (`lib/llm.rb:113-115`):
   ```ruby
   llm = LLM.openai(key: ENV["KEY"])  # Triggers require + new
   ```

2. **Provider initialization** (`lib/llm/provider.rb:29-40`):
   - Sets key, host, port, timeout, SSL
   - Configures transport layer
   - Creates Monitor for locking headers

3. **Call enters `complete` method** (`lib/llm/provider.rb:94-96` abstract):
   - OpenAI implementation at `lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:73-82`

4. **Parameter normalization** (`lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:210-220`):
   - Merge defaults, resolve tools, adapt schema
   - Extract role, stream flags

5. **Request building** (`lib/llm/providers/openai.rb:222-228`):
   - Build messages via `adapt()` → `RequestAdapter::Completion#adapt`
   - Create Transport::Request with POST to `/chat/completions`
   - Set body stream from JSON

6. **Execution** (`lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:31-42`):
   - Start tracer span
   - Call transport.request with optional stream parser
   - Handle response (parse or error)

7. **Response handling** (`lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:51-54`):
   - `handle_response` → `error_handler raise_error!` OR `parse_response`
   - `ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)` → extends Response with completion module

8. **Response adapter** (`lib/llm/providers/openai/response_adapter/completion.rb:1-140`):
   - Maps choices → messages
   - Extracts token usage, model name
   - Includes `LLM::Contract::Completion` interface

**For streaming**, same flow but `stream:` param passes Stream object, parser emits callbacks.

**Shared base contract (lines 55-185 in provider.rb):**

### **Step 5: Streaming and Error-Handling Paths**

**Streaming path (from provider complete method, e.g. openai.rb lines 73-82):**

1. **Provider detects stream** (`lib/llm/provider.rb:338-340`):
   ```ruby
   stream = params[:stream]
   params[:stream] = true if streamable?(stream) || stream == true
   ```

2. **Execute with stream parser** (`lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:31-42`):
   ```ruby
   stream &&= LLM::Object.from(streamer: stream, parser: stream_parser, decoder: stream_decoder)
   res = transport.request(request, owner:, stream:, &b)
   ```

3. **Stream parser processes chunks** (e.g., openai.rb lines 16-211):
   - `parse!(chunk)` merges chunks into `@body`
   - Emits callbacks: `on_content`, `on_reasoning_content`, `on_tool_call`
   - Resolves tools against request tools or global registry

4. **Stream interface** (`lib/llm/stream.rb:62-98`):
   - `on_content(content)` - Text chunks
   - `on_reasoning_content(content)` - Reasoning (OpenAI, Bedrock)
   - `on_tool_call(tool, error)` - Complete tool calls
   - `on_tool_return(tool, result)` - Tool execution results
   - Supports `#<<` as alias for `on_content`

**Error handling:**

1. **HTTP status detection** (`lib/llm/transport/response.rb:67-111`):
   - `ok?`, `unauthorized?`, `rate_limited?`, `not_found?`, `server_error?`

2. **Provider-specific error handling:**
   - **OpenAI** (`lib/llm/providers/openai/error_handler.rb`): Maps error types, handles invalid_request_error with context_length_exceeded → ContextWindowError
   - **Anthropic** (`lib/llm/providers/anthropic/error_handler.rb`): Basic HTTP status mapping, no error type details
   - **Ollama** (`lib/llm/providers/ollama/error_handler.rb`): Basic HTTP status mapping
   - **Google** (`lib/llm/providers/google/error_handler.rb`): Checks 400 bad_request for API_KEY_INVALID → UnauthorizedError
   - **Bedrock** (`lib/llm/providers/bedrock/error_handler.rb`): Extracts message from body JSON, handles `__type` field

3. **Shared error types** (`lib/llm/error.rb`):
   - `UnauthorizedError`, `RateLimitError`, `ServerError`
   - `NotFoundError`, `FormatError`, `PromptError`
   - `InvalidRequestError`, `ContextWindowError`
   - `ToolLoopError`, `GuardError`, `Interrupt`
   - `RactorError`, `NoSuchToolError`, `NoSuchModelError`
   - `LoadError`

4. **Error raising** (`lib/llm/transport/execution.rb:51-54`):
   ```ruby
   def handle_response(res, tracer, span)
     res.ok? ? res.body = parse_response(res) : error_handler.new(tracer, span, res).raise_error!
   end
   ```

**Provider implementation template:**

```ruby
# lib/llm/providers/my_provider.rb
module LLM
  class MyProvider < Provider
    require_relative "my_provider/error_handler"
    require_relative "my_provider/request_adapter"
    require_relative "my_provider/response_adapter"
    require_relative "my_provider/stream_parser"
    require_relative "my_provider/models"
    
    include RequestAdapter
    
    HOST = "api.myprovider.com"
    
    def initialize(key: nil, **)
      super(host: HOST, key:, **)
    end
    
    def name
      :my_provider
    end
    
    def complete(prompt, params = {})
      params, stream, tools, role = normalize_complete_params(params)
      req = build_complete_request(prompt, params, role)
      res, span, tracer = execute(request: req, stream: stream, operation: "chat", model: params[:model])
      res = ResponseAdapter.adapt(res, type: :completion)
        .extend(Module.new { define_method(:__tools__) { tools } })
      tracer.on_request_finish(operation: "chat", model: params[:model], res:, span:)
      res
    end
    
    def models
      LLM::MyProvider::Models.new(self)
    end
    
    def assistant_role
      "assistant"
    end
    
    def default_model
      "my-provider-model"
    end
    
    private
    
    def headers
      lock do
        (@headers || {}).merge(
          "Content-Type" => "application/json",
          "Authorization" => "Bearer #{@key}"
        )
      end
    end
    
    def stream_parser
      LLM::MyProvider::StreamParser
    end
    
    def error_handler
      LLM::MyProvider::ErrorHandler
    end
  end
end
```

**Response adapter template (minimal):**

```ruby
# lib/llm/providers/my_provider/response_adapter.rb
module LLM::MyProvider::ResponseAdapter
  require_relative "response_adapter/completion"
  require_relative "response_adapter/embedding"
  require_relative "response_adapter/models"
  
  module_function
  
  def adapt(res, type:)
    response = LLM::Response === res ? res : LLM::Response.new(res)
    response.extend(select(type))
  end
  
  def select(type)
    case type
    when :completion then LLM::MyProvider::ResponseAdapter::Completion
    when :embedding then LLM::MyProvider::ResponseAdapter::Embedding
    when :models then LLM::MyProvider::ResponseAdapter::Models
    else
      raise ArgumentError, "Unknown response adapter type: #{type.inspect}"
    end
  end
end
```
